title: About
text:
This site displays automated chess analytics for kaczor594 on chesscom.
Games are fetched daily via the Chess.com public API using a custom Python pipeline. Each game’s PGN is parsed and every move is analyzed with Stockfish (depth 15–25) to compute per-move evaluations and centipawn loss.

Total games in database: 5557
Fully analyzed games: 5548
Total moves analyzed: 370,631
Analysis runs daily via a scheduled launchd job

title: About
text:
Performance Metric: Average Centipawn Loss (ACPL)
The primary metric used across this site is Average Centipawn Loss (ACPL) — the mean centipawn loss per move in a game. Lower values indicate more accurate play.
\[
\text{ACPL} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \max(0,\; \text{cp\_loss}_i)
\]
where \(\text{cp\_loss}_i\) is the centipawn loss for move \(i\) (the difference in engine evaluation between the best move and the move played), and \(n\) is the total number of moves.
Rough benchmarks:



ACPL
Level




< 10
Very strong play


10–30
Solid play


30–60
Average club player


60–100
Beginner-level mistakes


> 100
Frequent blunders

title: About
text:
Game Phase Definitions
Phases are assigned by Stockfish during analysis:







Phase
Heuristic




Opening
Moves within known book theory or early development (typically moves 1–~10)


Middlegame
After opening development, pieces actively engaged


Endgame
Reduced material; typically when fewer than 7 non-pawn pieces remain

title: About
text:
Technology Stack

Data pipeline: Python (python-chess, requests)
Engine: Stockfish
Database: SQLite
Analytics: R (tidyverse, plotly, DBI)
Site: Quarto → GitHub Pages


Last updated: May 06, 2026 at 03:00 CEST
